message:
docs(plan): Enhance web export proposal with bv deep dive insights (v1.0→v1.1)

Comprehensive enhancement of the GitHub Pages web export proposal based on
detailed analysis of bv's (beads_viewer) production implementation. This update
incorporates proven patterns and technical insights from bv's web export system.

## New Sections Added

### Security Enhancements (Section 7.5-7.6)
- Content Security Policy with strict headers and wasm-unsafe-eval for WASM
- CSP directive explanations (default-src, script-src, frame-ancestors, etc.)
- DOMPurify sanitization configuration matching bv's settings
- Service Worker for CORS isolation with same-origin enforcement
- Cross-Origin Isolation headers (COOP/COEP) for SharedArrayBuffer on Cloudflare

### Database & Performance (Section 9.2.1-9.2.2)
- Pre-computed data files pattern: statistics.json, timeline.json for instant
  dashboard rendering without client-side SQL aggregation
- Materialized views: mv_recent_by_agent, mv_message_snippets for 10-15%
  size increase but dramatically faster query performance

### Multi-Tier Database Loading (Section 9.5)
- OPFS (Origin Private File System) caching that survives page refreshes
- Chunked downloads for databases >5MB (1MB chunks with SHA256 verification)
- Three-tier loading strategy: OPFS cache → chunked → single file
- Browser compatibility matrix for OPFS support

### WASM Memory Management (Section 9.6)
- withDatabaseScope() pattern for automatic resource cleanup (inspired by
  bv's withSubgraph() pattern)
- Hybrid WASM scorer that falls back to JS for small datasets (<5000 items)
- Memory budget monitoring to warn when approaching WASM heap limits

### Wizard Implementation Details (Section 8.1.1)
- Rust state machine using dialoguer + indicatif + console
- Dynamic ContentStats from database queries for live statistics
- Multi-step progress display matching bv's style
- Prerequisite checking (gh CLI, authentication, disk space)

## Enhanced Existing Sections

### FTS5 Search (Section 9.2)
- Changed tokenizer to 'porter unicode61' for better search quality
  (Porter stemmer: "running" matches "run", "runs", "runner")
- Added indexes for common query patterns (messages.conversation_id,
  conversations.agent, conversations.workspace, conversations.started_at)

### Frontend Technology Stack (Section 11)
- Updated library versions with accurate sizes (gzipped + Brotli columns)
- Added DOMPurify (20KB/8KB gz) for XSS sanitization
- Added optional libraries section: D3.js, Force-Graph, Mermaid
- Size breakdown by user journey (initial page → password → unlock)
- Bundle optimization strategies:
  - Code splitting with dynamic imports
  - Parallel WASM initialization
  - Critical CSS inlining
  - Asset preloading with link rel="preload"
- Enhanced browser compatibility table with OPFS/Service Worker columns
- Hard vs soft requirements (graceful degradation)

## Key Technical Insights from bv

1. OPFS caching provides instant loading on return visits (<50ms)
2. SHA256 verification per chunk prevents data corruption
3. Porter stemmer improves search recall significantly
4. Pre-computed analytics eliminate expensive client-side aggregation
5. wasm-unsafe-eval is required for sql.js/Argon2 WASM compilation
6. Hybrid scorer avoids WASM call overhead for small datasets
7. Service Worker provides additional security layer

Document grew from ~1,639 lines to ~2,479 lines (+879 insertions, -39 deletions)
with production-tested patterns from bv's implementation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
